zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Hugh de Vere 4th Earl of Oxford於1207年出生在英國艾可時，他的父親Robert de Vere 3rd Earl of Oxford42歲，母親Isabel de Bolebec43歲。他於1223年2月25日與Countess Hawise de Quincy在英國英格蘭哈普爾維古姆結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於1263年12月23日之前在英國艾可可爾內 恩蓋，埋葬在英國艾可易阿爾斯 可爾內。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Hugh de Vere 4th Earl of Oxford于1207年出生在英国艾可时，他的父亲Robert de Vere 3rd Earl of Oxford42岁，母亲Isabel de Bolebec43岁。他于1223年2月25日与Countess Hawise de Quincy在英国英格兰哈普爾維古姆结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他去世于1263年12月23日之前在英国艾可可爾內 恩蓋，埋葬在英国艾可易阿爾斯 可爾內。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Vere
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): habitational name, mostly from Vair in Ancenis, named with Gaulish ver(n)- ‘alder’. This was the probable home in the 11th century of the Breton lord Aubrey (Albericus) de Ver, who followed William the Conqueror to England and founded an influential family who held the earldom of Oxford until 1703. In a few cases the surname could be from other places in France, such as Ver in Manche and Calvados, which have the same etymology as Vair.
French (mainy Vendée; Veré): variant of Vairé, from Old French vairie ‘variegated, spotted’, applied as a nickname for someone who has freckles.

nameMeanings.name: Hugh
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French name, Hugues, Hugo, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from hug ‘heart, mind, spirit’. It was originally a short form of various compound names containing this element. This was borne by the aristocracy of medieval France, adopted by the Normans, and introduced by them to Britain. It was consistently one of the most popular boys' names in the Middle Ages in England, but fell out of favour in the 16th century until it was revived in the 20th century. Little Hugh of Lincoln was a child supposed in the Middle Ages to have been murdered by Jews in about 1255 , a legend responsible for several outbursts of anti-Semitism at various times. The story is referred to by Chaucer in The Prioress's Tale. He is not to be confused with St Hugh of Lincoln ( 1140–1200 ), bishop of Lincoln ( 1186–1200 ), who was noted for his charity and good works, his piety, and his defence of the Church against the State. Since the later Middle Ages Hugh has also been used as an Anglicized form of the Gaelic name Aodh .

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Hugh de Vere 4th Earl of Oxford was born in 1207, in Essex, England, his father, Robert de Vere 3rd Earl of Oxford, was 42 and his mother, Isabel de Bolebec, was 43. He married Countess Hawise de Quincy on 25 February 1223, in Winchester, Hampshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 3 daughters. He died before 23 December 1263, in Colne Engaine, Essex, England, and was buried in Earls Colne, Essex, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Hugh de Vere 4th Earl of Oxford est né en 1207, à Essex, Angleterre, son père, Robert de Vere 3rd Earl of Oxford, avait 42 ans et sa mère, Isabel de Bolebec, avait 43 ans. Il a épousé Countess Hawise de Quincy le 25 février 1223, à Winchester, Hampshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 3 filles. Il est décédé avant 23 décembre 1263, à Colne Engaine, Essex, Angleterre, et a été enterré à Earls Colne, Essex, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Hugh de Vere 4th Earl of Oxford 1207 in Essex, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Robert de Vere 3rd Earl of Oxford 42 und seine Mutter Isabel de Bolebec 43. Er hat am 25. Februar 1223 in Winchester, Hampshire, England, United Kingdom Countess Hawise de Quincy geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 3 Töchter. Er starb vor 23. Dezember 1263 in Colne Engaine, Essex, England und wurde in Earls Colne, Essex, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Hugh de Vere 4th Earl of Oxford è nato il nel 1207 nel Essex, England, il suo padre, Robert de Vere 3rd Earl of Oxford, aveva 42 anni e il suo madre, Isabel de Bolebec, aveva 43 anni. Lui sposato Countess Hawise de Quincy il 25 febbraio 1223, nel Winchester, Hampshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 3 figlie. Morì prima del 23 dicembre 1263, nel Colne Engaine, Essex, England, e fu sepolto a Earls Colne, Essex, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Hugh de Vere 4th Earl of Oxfordが1207年にイギリスエセックスで生まれた時，父Robert de Vere 3rd Earl of Oxfordは42歳，母Isabel de Bolebecは43歳であった。1223年2月25日にイギリスイギリスハンプシュアイアウィンチェスターでCountess Hawise de Quincyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼は1263年12月23日以前にイギリスエセックスコルン エンガインで死去し，イギリスエセックスエアルルズ コルンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Hugh de Vere 4th Earl of Oxford(이)가 1207년 영국 에세스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Robert de Vere 3rd Earl of Oxford(은)는 42세였고, 그의 어머니 Isabel de Bolebec(은)는 43세였다. 그는 1223년 2월 25일 영국 잉글랜드 함프실르 윈체스터에서 Countess Hawise de Quincy(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1263년 12월 23일 이전 영국 에세스 코니 잉가인에서 사망했으며 영국 에세스 에를스 코니에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Hugh de Vere 4th Earl of Oxford nasceu em 1207, em Essex, Inglaterra, seu pai, Robert de Vere 3rd Earl of Oxford, tinha 42 anos e sua mãe, Isabel de Bolebec, tinha 43 anos. Ele casou-se com Countess Hawise de Quincy em 25 de fevereiro de 1223, em Winchester, Hampshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 3 filhas. Ele faleceu antes 23 de dezembro de 1263, em Colne Engaine, Essex, Inglaterra, e foi sepultado em Earls Colne, Essex, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Hugh de Vere 4th Earl of Oxford родился в 1207 г. в местности эссекс, Англия, его отцу по имени Robert de Vere 3rd Earl of Oxford было 42 года, а матери, которую звали Isabel de Bolebec, 43. Он женился на женщине по имени Countess Hawise de Quincy 25 февраля 1223 г. в местности винчестир, хампшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 3 дочери. Он умер перед 23 декабрь 1263 г. в местности колн энгеин, эссекс, Англия и был похоронен в местности эарлс колн, эссекс, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir Hugh de Vere 4th Earl of Oxford nació en 1207, en Essex, Inglaterra, su papá, Robert de Vere 3rd Earl of Oxford, tenía 42 años y su mamá, Isabel de Bolebec, tenía 43 años. Se casó con Countess Hawise de Quincy el 25 de febrero de 1223, en Winchester, Hampshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 3 hijas. Murió antes de 23 de diciembre de 1263, en Colne Engaine, Essex, Inglaterra, y fue sepultado en Earls Colne, Essex, Inglaterra.
